title: Humboltia bilamellata
intro: Humboldtia bilamellata, also known as the Indian laburnum or tree of life, is a small, evergreen tree native to the Western Ghats mountain range in India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, cough, and digestive disorders, as well as having anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia bilamellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, and is used to make herbal teas, capsules, and topical creams to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Humboldtia bilamellata is classified within the family Sapindaceae. It is further categorized under the order Sapindales. The species belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Its classification at the family level is due to its morphological and anatomical characteristics being similar to those of other Sapindaceae family members. At the species level, it is recognized by its unique features and distinct taxonomy.
uses: Humboltia bilamellata has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's roots and leaves are often used in herbal remedies. In traditional medicine, Humboltia bilamellata is also used to treat digestive issues and as a treatment for certain types of infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential side effects. The plant's medicinal uses are largely based on its traditional applications and have not been extensively scientifically studied.

Its extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against infections and cell damage.
